POL-370.title: IMPLEMENT: Audit-enrich auto-mode Polaris runs (ledger completeness + telemetry signals)
POL-370.body:
## Objective

Add missing ledger events and telemetry signals so that auto-mode (parent-loop) Polaris runs are auditably trustworthy — a reviewer can verify from durable artifacts that real work was done, how long it took, and which files were changed.

## Context

Triggered by [POL-369](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-369/analyze-make-fast-polaris-runs-auditably-trustworthy) (ANALYZE: Make fast Polaris runs auditably trustworthy). PR 126 / [POL-352](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352/implement-route-identity-model-route-health-schema-and-welfare-checks) completed in \~2.7 minutes across 3 workers using the terminal-cli/devin adapter. Analysis revealed two critical gaps:

1. **Ledger gap**: Auto-mode parent-loop runs write only `run-started` to the ledger. No `child-completed` or `cluster-complete` events are ever written. Manual-flow runs (via `polaris loop continue`) correctly write `child-completed` events in `continue.ts`. The auto flow has no equivalent.
2. **Telemetry gap**: The `child-complete` telemetry event records `validation_summary` and `commit_hash`, but not elapsed time since dispatch or the list of files the commit touched. There is no way to tell from telemetry how long a worker ran, or which files it actually changed.

Evidence: `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl` for [POL-352](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352/implement-route-identity-model-route-health-schema-and-welfare-checks) has exactly one event (`run-started`); telemetry shows workers completing in 44–61 seconds with no timing field.

## Goal

After implementation: every auto-mode Polaris cluster run writes `child-completed` and `cluster-complete` ledger events; every `child-complete` telemetry event includes `elapsed_seconds` and `commit_files`.

## Scope

* src/loop/parent.ts
* src/loop/ledger.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `child-completed` ledger event written after each child completes in auto mode
- [ ] `cluster-complete` ledger event written when all children are done in auto mode
- [ ] `child-complete` telemetry event includes `elapsed_seconds` (integer, seconds since dispatch)
- [ ] `child-complete` telemetry event includes `commit_files` (string\[\] or null)
- [ ] All existing tests pass; new tests cover the new events and fields

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

None — standalone cluster, no upstream dependencies.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the manual-flow (`continue.ts`) ledger path
* Do not change worker result schema
* Do not add scope alignment cross-checking (future work)
* Do not add minimum elapsed time gating (future work)

POL-372.title: Add elapsed_seconds and commit_files to child-complete telemetry event
POL-372.body:
## Objective

Enrich the `child-complete` telemetry event with `elapsed_seconds` (time since dispatch) and `commit_files` (files touched by the worker's commit) so a reviewer can verify from telemetry alone that a fast run did real work.

## Context

The `child-complete` telemetry event (`src/loop/parent.ts` lines \~1587–1595) currently emits `validation_summary` and `commit_hash`, but nothing about how long the worker ran or which files it changed.

For PR 126 / [POL-352](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352/implement-route-identity-model-route-health-schema-and-welfare-checks), workers completed in 44–61 seconds each. There is no field in telemetry to surface this; `elapsed_seconds` would immediately make the timing visible to reviewers.

The artifact-only gate (`parent.ts` lines \~1424–1493) already computes `commitFiles` via `getCommitFiles(lastCommit, repoRoot)` inside a scoped block, but discards the list after the gate check. Lifting `commitFiles` out of the block scope and adding it to the `child-complete` event costs almost nothing and produces a durable file-level audit trail.

`dispatch_record.dispatched_at` is already stored in `state.open_children_meta[nextChild].dispatch_record.dispatched_at` — no new fields are needed in the dispatch record.

Source: [POL-369](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-369/analyze-make-fast-polaris-runs-auditably-trustworthy) → [POL-370](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-370/implement-audit-enrich-auto-mode-polaris-runs-ledger-completeness).

## Goal

After this child: the `child-complete` telemetry event in `telemetry.jsonl` includes:

* `elapsed_seconds: number` — integer seconds from `dispatch_record.dispatched_at` to the moment the event is emitted. Omitted (not zero) if `dispatched_at` is unavailable.
* `commit_files: string[] | null` — the resolved file list from `getCommitFiles`, or `null` if the commit was unresolvable or absent (matching existing fail-open behavior).

## Scope

* src/loop/parent.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `child-complete` telemetry event includes `elapsed_seconds` (integer) when `dispatch_record.dispatched_at` is available
- [ ] `elapsed_seconds` is omitted (not set to 0) when `dispatch_record.dispatched_at` is unavailable
- [ ] `child-complete` telemetry event includes `commit_files` (string\[\] or null)
- [ ] `commit_files` is `null` when `getCommitFiles` returns null (fail-open, no gate behavior change)
- [ ] The artifact-only gate logic is NOT changed — only the output telemetry is enriched
- [ ] Tests verify `elapsed_seconds` and `commit_files` are present in the `child-complete` event

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

None — structurally independent of Child 1.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify the artifact-only gate logic
* Do not add `elapsed_seconds` or `commit_files` to ledger events (ledger schema unchanged)
* Do not add scope alignment cross-checking
* Do not gate on minimum elapsed time (warn only via the new field, no halt)

POL-371.title: Emit child-completed and cluster-complete ledger events from parent-loop auto flow
POL-371.body:
## Objective

Add missing `child-completed` and `cluster-complete` ledger events to the auto-mode parent-loop execution path so the run ledger reflects every child completion in auto runs.

## Context

Auto-mode Polaris runs (using terminal-cli or similar adapters via `parent.ts`) call `appendRunStartedLedgerEvent` at run start but never write `child-completed` or `cluster-complete` ledger events. The manual-flow (`polaris loop continue` via `continue.ts`) correctly calls `writeContinueLedgerEvents` after each child, writing `child-completed` to `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl`. The auto flow has no equivalent.

Evidence: `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl` for [POL-352](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-352/implement-route-identity-model-route-health-schema-and-welfare-checks) (an auto-mode run with 3 children) contains exactly one event (`run-started`). [POL-331](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-331/implement-june-5-doctrine-gaps-medic-charts-navigation-before) (manual flow) contains `child-completed` events for each child. The ledger is the cross-agent resume authority; without these events, auto-mode run outcomes cannot be verified from the ledger alone.

Source: [POL-369](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-369/analyze-make-fast-polaris-runs-auditably-trustworthy) → [POL-370](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-370/implement-audit-enrich-auto-mode-polaris-runs-ledger-completeness).

## Goal

After this child: when `runParentLoop` in `src/loop/parent.ts` completes each child (whether via `advanceState` or worker-wrote-completion path), it appends a `child-completed` ledger event. When the cluster finishes, it appends a `cluster-complete` ledger event. Reuse the existing `LedgerWriter`, `ChildCompletedEvent`, and `ClusterCompleteEvent` types from `src/loop/ledger.ts`.

## Scope

* src/loop/parent.ts
* src/loop/ledger.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After each child completes in auto mode, a `child-completed` event appears in `.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl` with correct `run_id`, `cluster_id`, `branch`, `issue_id`, `last_commit`, `completed_children`, `open_children`, `validation`
- [ ] After all children complete (cluster done), a `cluster-complete` event appears in the ledger
- [ ] Dry-run mode (`dryRun: true`) does not write ledger events
- [ ] Tests in `src/loop/` verify ledger events are written on child completion in auto mode

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop

## Ordering

None — can execute first in the cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify `continue.ts` or its ledger path (leave the manual flow unchanged)
* Do not add new `LedgerEvent` types (reuse existing `ChildCompletedEvent` and `ClusterCompleteEvent`)
* Do not add `elapsed_seconds` to ledger events (that is Child 2 scope via telemetry)

blocker.reason: POL-371 sealed result failed CompactReturn validation: next_recommended_action must be continue, stop, or investigate; worker emitted proceed_to_next_child
